title: Beignets
cScaleSummary: C0 because the recipe contains no cholesterol-contributing animal-derived ingredients. The flour, yeast, date syrup, olive oil, vanilla, salt, water, and powdered sugar contribute 0 mg cholesterol.
ldlActiveSummary: Not LDL-Active because this is a refined-flour breakfast sweet with a powdered sugar finish, even though it is baked and cholesterol-free. It fits EWHF as an occasional fun breakfast recipe, not as an actively LDL-lowering dish.
intro: Beignets are one of the signature breakfast sweets of New Orleans, especially tied to the French Market coffee-and-beignet tradition. Classic versions are yeasted dough squares fried until puffed, then covered with powdered sugar. This version keeps the New Orleans breakfast idea: hot, soft, airy dough with a lightly crisp exterior and a powdered sugar finish, but moves the cooking method to the oven. It is not pretending to be a fried beignet. It is a lighter, cholesterol-free baked version that still belongs with strong coffee.

stepGroups.title: Make the Dough
items: Combine the warm water ({{quantity:warm-water}}), yeast ({{quantity:active-dry-yeast}}), and date syrup ({{quantity:date-syrup}}) in a mixing bowl.
Let stand for 5 to 10 minutes, until the yeast looks foamy.
Add the flour ({{quantity:all-purpose-flour}}), fine sea salt ({{quantity:fine-sea-salt}}), olive oil ({{quantity:olive-oil-dough}}), and vanilla ({{quantity:vanilla-extract}}).
Mix until a soft dough forms.
Knead for 5 to 6 minutes, until the dough is smooth, elastic, and slightly tacky but not wet.

stepGroups.title: First Rise
items: Place the dough in a lightly oiled bowl.
Cover and let rise in a warm place for 60 to 75 minutes, until doubled.

stepGroups.title: Shape the Beignets
items: Turn the dough onto a lightly floured surface.
Roll into a rectangle about 1/2 inch thick.
Cut into 12 squares.
Transfer the squares to a parchment-lined sheet pan, leaving space between them.

stepGroups.title: Second Rise
items: Cover loosely.
Let rise for 25 to 35 minutes, until slightly puffed.

stepGroups.title: Bake
items: Heat the oven to 425°F.
Brush the tops lightly with the olive oil ({{quantity:olive-oil-finish}}).
Bake for 9 to 12 minutes, until puffed, lightly browned at the edges, and cooked through.
For a little more surface color, leave them in for 1 to 2 minutes longer, but do not dry them out.

stepGroups.title: Finish
items: Let the beignets cool for 2 minutes.
Dust with powdered sugar ({{quantity:powdered-sugar}}) while still warm.
Serve immediately with strong coffee.
